etymology.description: 有(有) (yǒu, "have; there is"), 位(位) (wèi, "position; seat"), 子(子) (zi, "noun suffix"), 嗎(嗎) (ma, "question particle")
etymology.interpretation: 有(有) (yǒu, "have; there is") suggests possession or existence, implying the presence of something. 位(位) (wèi, "position; seat") denotes a specific location or available spot, often related to seating or rank. 子(子) (zi, "noun suffix") creates a noun phrase, finalizing the concept of a 'seat.' 嗎(嗎) (ma, "question particle") turns the phrase into a question, asking whether such a seat exists.

examples.chinese: 餐厅里的人很多(餐廳裡的人很多)，你可以问一下有位子吗(有位子嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Cāntīng lǐ de rén hěn duō, nǐ kěyǐ wèn yīxià yǒu wèi zi ma?
examples.english: There are many people in the restaurant, can you ask if there is a seat available?

examples.chinese: 请问这里有位子吗(請問這裡有位子嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Qǐng wèn zhèlǐ yǒu wèi zi ma?
examples.english: Excuse me, is there a seat available here?

examples.chinese: 如果你去电影院，你应该先查有位子吗(有位子嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ qù diànyǐngyuàn, nǐ yīnggāi xiān chá yǒu wèi zi ma?
examples.english: If you go to the cinema, you should check first if there is a seat available.
